issuesFound.title: Mobile nav toggle missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open, .close, and .toggle functions change the visibility of the navigation drawer but do not update the aria-expanded state on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed of the menu's open/closed state, breaking WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2000: In the open function, add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`. Line 2010: In the close function, add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`. Line 2020: In the toggle function, update aria-expanded based on the new state.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance and _closeInstance functions show/hide dropdown panels without setting aria-expanded on the trigger buttons. Screen reader users cannot perceive the expanded/collapsed state of these menus, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2100: In _openInstance, add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`. Line 2115: In _closeInstance, add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Search suggestion panel toggle missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch._open function displays the search suggestion listbox but does not set aria-expanded on the search input. Screen reader users are not notified that the popup is open, breaking WCAG 4.1.2 for comboboxes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2200: In _open, add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`. Also update the _close function to set `aria-expanded="false"`.

issuesFound.title: Form validation missing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The inlineValidator._renderResult function marks fields as invalid by adding CSS classes but does not set aria-invalid="true". Screen reader users are not alerted to the error state of the field, failing WCAG 3.3.1 and 4.1.2.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on global :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible rule removes the outline and does not add any visible focus indicator (e.g., box‑shadow or border). Keyboard users cannot see which element has focus, violating WCAG 2.4.7.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger buttons missing aria‑expanded on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The buttons that open the dropdown menus (selected by `SELECTORS.navDropdownTrigger = '#nav-menu > li > button[aria-haspopup]'`) are never given an `aria‑expanded` attribute when the page loads. Without this attribute screen‑reader users cannot know whether the associated submenu is collapsed or expanded.
issuesFound.fix:
In the `register(trigger, panel)` function (around line 2152) add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after the `aria-controls` assignment.

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑navigation toggle button missing aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button (`SELECTORS.navToggle = '.nav-toggle'`) that opens and closes the mobile navigation drawer never receives an `aria‑expanded` attribute, so assistive technologies cannot convey its state.
issuesFound.fix:
In `mobileNav.init()` (around line 1478) after locating `_trigger`, add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`. Update the `open()` and `close()` functions to set the attribute to `"true"` and `"false"` respectively.

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger buttons missing aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ items use buttons (`[data-faq-trigger]`) to show/hide answers, but the code never sets `aria‑expanded` on those buttons when the page loads, leaving their collapsed state undisclosed to AT.
issuesFound.fix:
In `faqRegistry.parse()` (around line 2624) after `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-controls', panelId);` add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`. Then in `faqAccordion.open()` and `close()` toggle the attribute appropriately.

---

issuesFound.title: Simple billing toggle (annual/monthly) missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The on/off switch (`[data-billing-toggle]`) that toggles between monthly and annual billing is given `role="switch"` but never receives an `aria‑pressed` attribute, so its state is not announced.
issuesFound.fix:
In `pricing.init()` (around line 3521) after setting `role="switch"` add `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', 'false');`. Then in `pricing._onToggleClick()` update the attribute to `"true"`/`"false"` accordingly.

---

issuesFound.title: Form validation does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The `inlineValidator` module validates fields and displays error messages, but it never adds `aria-invalid="true"` to inputs that fail validation (and never removes it on success). Assistive technologies therefore cannot detect the error state.
issuesFound.fix:
In `inlineValidator._renderResult()` (around line 1912) after marking the field as invalid, add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` and on the valid branch remove the attribute (`removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');`).

---

issuesFound.title: Dynamic product‑filter count updates are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the visible product count is updated in `_updateCountBadge(count)`, but the change is not communicated to screen‑reader users because no ARIA live region is used.
issuesFound.fix:
Add a hidden live region element (e.g., `
`) to the page and, in `_updateCountBadge(count)`, set its text content to `${count} product${count !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`. This will announce the new count. ---

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content changes (e.g., pricing period label, view‑mode toggle) lack live‑region announcements
issuesFound.explanation: Functions such as `pricing.setPeriod()` and the view‑mode toggle in `productFilter._onViewToggle()` change visible text but do not write to any element with `role="status"` or `aria-live`. Users of assistive technology are not informed of these changes.
issuesFound.fix:
Create a generic live region (e.g., `
`) and, after each state change, update its text (e.g., `Pricing switched to annual billing` or `View mode changed to list`). Insert the update code at the end of `pricing.setPeriod()` and `productFilter._onViewToggle()`. ---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: `dropdownMenu._openInstance()` and `_closeInstance()` add/remove the `hidden` attribute on the panel but never set `aria‑expanded` on the trigger button, leaving the disclosure state invisible to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: In `_openInstance()` call `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` and in `_closeInstance()` call `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period buttons missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing module visually marks the selected period button with a CSS class but does not reflect the selected state via `aria‑pressed`, which is required for toggle‑style buttons.
issuesFound.fix: In `_syncToggleState()` after toggling the CSS class, add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(btnPeriod === period));` for each button.

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period change not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: Changing the billing period updates price numbers but does not send any announcement to a live region, so users of assistive technology are unaware that the pricing has changed.
issuesFound.fix: After updating all price elements in `setPeriod()`, call `liveRegion.announce(\`Billing period switched to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label}\`);`.

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors not reflected via aria‑invalid
issuesFound.explanation: `inlineValidator.validate()` adds an error message and CSS classes but never sets `aria‑invalid="true"` on the invalid field, so screen readers cannot detect the error state.
issuesFound.fix: After detecting an invalid result, add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');`.

issuesFound.title: Form validation does not clear aria‑invalid on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: `inlineValidator.clearField()` removes the error message and CSS classes but does not remove the `aria‑invalid` attribute, leaving the field incorrectly marked as invalid for assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: In `clearField()` add `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger missing initial aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown is registered, the trigger receives `aria-haspopup` and `aria-controls` but no initial `aria-expanded="false"`, violating the ARIA disclosure pattern.
issuesFound.fix: In `register()` after setting `aria-controls`, add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑navigation trigger missing initial aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button (`_trigger`) used to open the mobile navigation drawer is not given an initial `aria-expanded="false"` attribute, making its state ambiguous to assistive technology on page load.
issuesFound.fix: In `mobileNav.init()` after obtaining `_trigger`, add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open/close not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion.open() and close() change visibility but do not send an announcement to a live region, leaving AT users unaware of which answer is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Add liveRegion.announce(`Answer opened: ${item.question}`) in open(); and liveRegion.announce(`Answer closed: ${item.question}`) in close().

issuesFound.title: Inline form validation does not set aria-invalid on error
issuesFound.explanation: inlineValidator.validate() marks fields as invalid visually but never adds aria-invalid="true", so assistive technologies cannot detect the error state.
issuesFound.fix: In inlineValidator.validate(): if (!result.valid) setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true'); else removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');

issuesFound.title: Inline form validation does not clear aria-invalid on success
issuesFound.explanation: When a field becomes valid, the code removes error classes but does not remove aria-invalid, leaving stale invalid state for AT.
issuesFound.fix: In the else branch of inlineValidator.validate(): removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on mobile nav trigger at page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button starts with no aria-expanded attribute, violating the requirement that a collapsible widget expose its default state.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.init(): setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on dropdown triggers at page load
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown trigger buttons are created without an initial aria-expanded="false", so screen readers cannot know they are collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: In dropdownMenu.register(): setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu panels are shown and hidden, but the trigger buttons do not have their aria-expanded attribute updated. This prevents assistive technologies from announcing the state of the dropdown (expanded or collapsed).
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1910 (in _openInstance): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` after `inst.open = true;`
  Line 1925 (in _closeInstance): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` after `inst.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: Form validation missing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When form fields are validated, invalid fields are not marked with aria-invalid="true" and valid fields do not clear the aria-invalid attribute. Screen readers cannot convey the validation state of the fields to users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1240 (in markInvalid): Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');` after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`
  Line 1255 (in markValid): Add `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);` before or after the existing aria-describedby cleanup logic

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu triggers missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown trigger buttons are registered with aria-haspopup and aria-controls, but aria-expanded is not initialized to "false" for collapsed dropdowns on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2100 (after register(trigger, panel) calls): Add code to set initial aria-expanded: `qsa('[data-dropdown] button[aria-haspopup]').forEach(btn => setAttr(btn, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false'));` or modify register function to include `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` when registering closed dropdowns

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation state changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation menu opens or closes, no announcement is made to screen reader users via a live region. Users are not informed that the menu has opened or that they can now navigate within it.

  Line 1800 (in close): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed);` after closing the menu

issuesFound.title: Product filter results not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied and the product count updates, the change is not announced to screen readers. Users do not receive feedback on how many products match the selected filters.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2930 (after updating textContent): Add `liveRegion.announce(`${count} products found`);` or use the existing STRINGS: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(_productFilterState.activeCategory));`

issuesFound.title: Pricing price changes not announced

issuesFound.title: TextInput component missing aria-invalid mapping
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component accepts an invalid prop but does not map it to aria-invalid on the input element. Screen readers cannot announce the invalid state to users when form validation fails.
issuesFound.fix: Line 780: `<input id={id} aria-invalid={invalid ? "true" : undefined} className={['form-input', invalid ? 'form-input--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest} />`

issuesFound.title: Textarea component missing aria-invalid mapping
issuesFound.explanation: The Textarea component accepts an invalid prop but does not apply aria-invalid to the textarea element. Screen reader users are not notified when the field contains invalid input.
issuesFound.fix: Line 800: `<textarea id={id} aria-invalid={invalid ? "true" : undefined} className={['form-textarea', invalid ? 'form-textarea--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest} />`

issuesFound.title: Select component missing aria-invalid mapping
issuesFound.explanation: The Select component accepts an invalid prop but does not map it to aria-invalid on the select element. This prevents assistive technologies from announcing error states.
issuesFound.fix: Line 820: `<select id={id} aria-invalid={invalid ? "true" : undefined} className={['form-select', invalid ? 'form-select--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest} >`

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle annual button missing aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The annual billing toggle button manages selection state but lacks aria-pressed, preventing screen reader users from knowing which option is selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1670: `<button type="button" id="billing-annual" aria-pressed={value === 'annual'} className={['billing-toggle__btn', value === 'annual' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} onClick={() => onChange('annual')} >`

issuesFound.title: FormGroup required marker not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The visual asterisk marker for required fields is not marked with aria-hidden="true", causing screen readers to announce "star" redundantly since the "(required)" text is already provided via VisuallyHidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 760: ` *`

issuesFound.title: PlanCard article missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The PlanCard renders an article element without aria-labelledby, despite having a heading inside. Screen reader users navigating by landmark or article will encounter an unnamed region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1740: `<article aria-labelledby={headingId} className="pricing-card__article">`

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The primary navigation element lacks an aria-label. Since multiple nav elements exist on the page, users cannot distinguish this as the main navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1010: `<nav id="main-nav" aria-label="Main" className="desktop-nav">`

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav current page link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The current page link in desktop navigation uses a CSS class to indicate selection but lacks aria-current="page". Screen readers cannot identify this as the current location.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1020: `<a href={item.href} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} className={['nav-link', item.current ? 'nav-link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} >`

issuesFound.title: MobileNav current page link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The current page link in mobile navigation has a visual indicator class but no aria-current attribute, preventing screen reader users from identifying their current location.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1070: `<a href={item.href} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} className={['mobile-nav__link', item.current ? 'mobile-nav__link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} onClick={onClose} >`

issuesFound.title: StarRating decorative symbols not hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The star symbols (★ and ☆) are rendered inside a span that provides the accessible name, but the individual star spans are not marked aria-hidden="true", causing redundant or confusing announcements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 640: `<span key={i} aria-hidden="true" className={filled ? 'star star--filled' : 'star star--empty'}>`

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown chevron icon not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The chevron indicator (▲/▼) inside the dropdown trigger is decorative since the button already has text content, but it lacks aria-hidden="true", causing screen readers to announce the triangle symbols.
issuesFound.fix: Line 985: `<span className="nav-link__chevron" aria-hidden="true">`

issuesFound.title: Button component missing aria-busy during loading
issuesFound.explanation: When the Button component is in a loading state, it sets disabled and shows a spinner but does not set aria-busy="true". Screen reader users are not informed that the button is processing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 720: `<button type={type} disabled={isDisabled} aria-disabled={isDisabled || undefined} aria-busy={isLoading ? "true" : undefined} className={['btn', `btn--${variant}`, `btn--${size}`, className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest} >`
